name: Paul Ingram
story_relevance_note: Tucson Sentinel masthead lists Ingram as senior reporter on border/immigration and politics/public policy. The Mayes lawsuit against DHS over the Surprise ICE facility hits both beats squarely (NEPA/INA claims, Rinchem chlorine and HF storage proximity). Direct fit for an investigative tip on the fourth-state fact pattern after MD, NJ, and MI.
personalization_hook: Saw your March 27, 2026 story on the federal court ending the Center for Biological Diversity lawsuit over border barrier work in Southern Arizona wildland.

name: Dylan Smith
story_relevance_note: As editor and co-publisher Smith routes immigration and AG-litigation stories at the AZ-based nonprofit Sentinel. Worth pitching the underlying federal-records analysis (Northwest Valley Indivisible's Rinchem chemical-zone documentation) so the Sentinel can run a deeper companion piece to the lawsuit filing.
personalization_hook: Saw your "2025 in review: Tucson Sentinel's top stories of the year" covering immigration and asylum in Southern Arizona.

name: Blake Morlock
story_relevance_note: Morlock writes the 'What the Devil won't tell you' and 'Tucson Agenda' columns covering AZ political accountability. The Mayes filing against DHS plus the city of Surprise council fight over a 542-bed facility next to a chlorine warehouse fits his column register: a state-vs-feds confrontation with documented public-safety risk.
personalization_hook: Saw your "What the Devil won't tell you" column on Tucson voters rejecting the half-cent sales tax on Apr 16, 2026.

name: Jim Nintzel
story_relevance_note: Nintzel is the Sentinel's government-impact reporter and a Report for America corps member. Mayes suing DHS is the rare instance where AZ AG, federal courts, and a municipal council collide on one fact pattern. Send him the Maryland injunction precedent and the Rinchem RMP records.
personalization_hook: Saw your "Press Room: ICE in South Tucson, budget challenges for Tucson" report on April 10, 2026.

name: Adrian O'Farrill
story_relevance_note: O'Farrill is listed on the Sentinel masthead as the 2025 Reporting Fellow from Craig Newmark / CUNY J-Corps. Fellows take handed-up assignments. The Surprise facility location data plus federal-records audit is a discrete reporting package a fellow can develop quickly.
personalization_hook: Saw your contribution to "2025 in review: Tucson Sentinel's top stories of the year" on increased civic coverage.

name: Daniel Shailer
role: Reporting Fellow (Institute for Nonprofit News / Columbia)
story_relevance_note: Listed on Sentinel masthead as INN/Columbia reporting fellow. The 8,000+ public comments filed against the Surprise facility plus the No-ICE-in-Surprise community coalition give him a documents-and-people story to work.

name: Tucson Sentinel Border Desk
story_relevance_note: Sentinel publishes border@tucsonsentinel.com as the dedicated tip line for border and immigration tips. Fits the AG's INA-violation claim and the broader detention-buildout pattern across MD, NJ, MI, and now AZ.

name: Tucson Sentinel Crime Desk
story_relevance_note: The chemical-warehouse proximity (Rinchem chlorine and hydrofluoric acid storage across the street from a planned 542-bed detention facility) is a public-safety story before it's a politics story. The crime desk routes EPA RMP and emergency-response material.
personalization_hook: Saw your April 24 coverage of Arizona AG Mayes' lawsuit to block the ICE facility near the chemical warehouse in Surprise.

name: Maria Coxon-Smith
story_relevance_note: Co-Publisher and Engagement Editor at Tucson Sentinel. Engagement editors decide which civic-action stories the newsroom rallies around. The 8,000+ public-comment count and the May 5 Surprise council meeting give a clear newsroom hook.

name: Natalie Robbins
story_relevance_note: Robbins covers 'Creative Community Solutions' for the Sentinel. The No-ICE-in-Surprise coalition and Northwest Valley Indivisible's federal-records work are exactly the volunteer-civic-mobilization stories on her beat.
personalization_hook: Saw your award-winning reporting on the **Tucson Community Bail Fund** that took first place in solutions journalism.

name: Mia Kortright
role: Cultural Expression and Community Values Reporter
story_relevance_note: Sentinel staff page lists Kortright on community values. The Surprise residents organizing against a detention-and-chemical-zone collision is a community-values story with quotable named neighbors and a documented fight.
personalization_hook: Saw your "Center for Cultural Organizing opens at former Tucson Chinese market" piece in Tucson Sentinel on April 19.

name: Kiara Adams
story_relevance_note: Sentinel masthead lists Adams as 2025 Reporting Fellow from the University of Arizona. The Marana ICE facility complement to Surprise (Senators Kelly and Gallego flagged both in their 2026-04-17 DHS letter) is a fellow-sized assignment.

name: Ruby McKeown
story_relevance_note: Listed as 2024-25 Sentinel/Arizona Media Association Reporting Fellow on the Sentinel masthead. Fellows handle developing-story turnarounds; the bed-count drop (1,500 claimed to 542 beds) is a focused accountability angle.

name: Cris Seda Chabrier
story_relevance_note: Sentinel masthead lists Seda Chabrier as 2024 Reporting Fellow from Craig Newmark / CUNY. The fact pattern (federal-state-municipal collision over a single parcel) is a teachable case study in NEPA litigation that fits a fellow's reporting pace.
personalization_hook: Saw your October 6, 2024 piece "Arizona's ballot Prop. 314 would further criminalize migration" in Tucson Sentinel.

name: Aliento Media
story_relevance_note: Aliento is Reyna Montoya's AZ-based immigrant-led advocacy organization (Phoenix HQ). Their press email is the public route to a quote on the human stakes of a 542-bed detention buildout in Surprise. Their constituency overlaps with the Mayes complaint's INA claims.

name: Florence Immigrant and Refugee Rights Project Media
outlet: Florence Immigrant and Refugee Rights Project (FIRRP)
story_relevance_note: FIRRP runs legal services inside Arizona ICE detention. They have first-hand visibility on detention conditions and standing to comment on a new 542-bed facility next to chemical storage. The lawsuit's NEPA claim about emergency-evacuation feasibility is exactly their lane.
personalization_hook: Saw your pilot universal representation model launch for merits-blind legal services to people in immigration detention facing deportation.

name: Florence Immigrant and Refugee Rights Project General
outlet: Florence Immigrant and Refugee Rights Project (FIRRP)
story_relevance_note: FIRRP's public general inbox. Fallback if media@ doesn't route. As Arizona's primary detention-legal-services nonprofit, the org is a named secondary source for any story on the Surprise or Marana facilities.
personalization_hook: Saw your launch of the pilot universal representation model for free, merits-blind legal services to people in immigration detention facing deportation.

name: Gabriela Millard
outlet: Florence Immigrant and Refugee Rights Project (FIRRP)
story_relevance_note: Direct staff contact published on FIRRP's contact page. Worth a direct outreach offering the federal-records analysis behind the Mayes complaint, since FIRRP's case load gives them standing to comment on conditions of confinement at any new AZ facility.
